lesson.title: Reading Strategies — Tasks 1-2
points: Read paragraph by paragraph — don't skim the whole text first
For each paragraph, check: can I answer any open question? YES → select, NO → move on
In Task 2, find the PATTERN in the diagram first, then read the email
Focus on MAIN IDEAS, not small details like exact dates or statistics
Part 2 (reply) is usually easier — you already know the context from Part 1

exercises.passage:
Dear Marcus,

I hope you're doing well! I wanted to let you know that our annual camping trip has been moved from August to September this year. The campground we usually go to is fully booked in August due to a music festival.

The good news is that September weather in Banff is actually perfect for hiking — cooler temperatures and fewer mosquitoes. I've already reserved three campsites for September 14-16.
exercises.question: Why was the camping trip moved to September?
options: The weather is too hot in August
The campground is booked due to a music festival
Marcus requested a different date
The hiking trails are closed in August
exercises.explanation: Technique: Paragraph-by-Paragraph. Paragraph 1 directly states "fully booked in August due to a music festival." Focus on the REASON given in the text, not assumptions.

exercises.passage:
I know you mentioned wanting to bring your new tent — the four-person one you bought last month. That would be great since Sarah and I can share the second campsite, and Mike said he'd bring his own gear. Could you also bring the portable stove? Mine broke during our last trip.

Let me know if September 14-16 works for you. If not, we might be able to push it to the following weekend.

Cheers,
Tom
exercises.question: What does Tom ask Marcus to bring besides the tent?
options: A sleeping bag for Sarah
The portable stove
Extra food for the group
A map of the hiking trails
exercises.explanation: Technique: Read for specific requests. "Could you also bring the portable stove? Mine broke." Focus on what is explicitly asked, not implied.

exercises.passage:
Hi Tom,

September works perfectly for me! Actually, I prefer it — I always get sunburned in August anyway. I'll bring the four-person tent and the portable stove. I also have a brand new cooler that keeps ice for three days, so I'll throw that in too.

One question: is there cell reception at the campground? I'm on call for work that weekend but I could ___.
exercises.question: Which best completes the blank in Marcus's reply?
options: cancel the trip entirely if there's no signal
bring a satellite phone just in case
ask my manager to take me off the on-call schedule
probably manage if I can check my phone occasionally
exercises.explanation: Technique: Reply Context. Marcus is going on the trip (confirmed "September works perfectly") and just has a small concern about being on call. He's not cancelling or bringing special equipment — he just needs occasional phone access.

exercises.passage:
Dear Tenants of Building 7,

Please be advised that scheduled maintenance will take place on Monday, March 24th. The water supply will be shut off between 9:00 AM and 2:00 PM while our team replaces the main pipe on the third floor.

We recommend filling containers with water before 9:00 AM for drinking and cooking needs. The maintenance should not affect electricity or heating.
exercises.question: What service will be unavailable during the maintenance?
options: Electricity throughout the building
Water supply from 9 AM to 2 PM
Heating in all apartments
Elevator access on the third floor
exercises.explanation: Technique: Focus on specific details when directly stated. "Water supply will be shut off between 9:00 AM and 2:00 PM." The text explicitly says electricity and heating will NOT be affected.

exercises.passage:
If you are working from home that day and need access to water, the community room on the ground floor will have bottled water available free of charge. Additionally, the gym showers will remain operational as they are on a separate water line.

We apologize for the inconvenience. This repair is necessary to prevent a potential pipe burst that could cause significant damage to multiple units.

Thank you for your understanding,
Building Management
exercises.question: Why is this maintenance being done?
options: Tenants complained about low water pressure
To install a new swimming pool
To prevent a potential pipe burst that could damage units
The city requires annual pipe inspections
exercises.explanation: Technique: Find the WHY. "This repair is necessary to prevent a potential pipe burst that could cause significant damage." Only select what the text says — no assumptions.

exercises.passage:
Dear Building Management,

Thank you for the advance notice. I work from home on Mondays, so I appreciate knowing about the bottled water in the community room. I have one concern: I have two elderly cats who need fresh water throughout the day. Would it be possible to ___?
exercises.question: Which best completes the tenant's request?
options: cancel the maintenance until a more convenient day
get a small supply of bottled water delivered to my unit
have the maintenance team work only at night instead
install a separate water line for my apartment
exercises.explanation: Technique: Reply logical flow. The tenant accepted the maintenance (thanked for notice) and has a specific small concern (cats need water). A small accommodation (bottled water to unit) fits the polite, reasonable tone.

exercises.passage:
Dear Professor Chen,

I hope this email finds you well. I'm writing to ask if you would be willing to serve as a reference for a job I'm applying to. The position is a Junior Data Analyst role at a financial technology company in Toronto.

I took your Statistics and Data Modeling courses in my third and fourth years, and I believe your recommendation would carry significant weight given the quantitative nature of the role.
exercises.question: What is the relationship between the writer and Professor Chen?
options: They are colleagues at the same company
The writer was Professor Chen's student
They are family friends
Professor Chen is the writer's current supervisor
exercises.explanation: Technique: Identify WHO and RELATIONSHIP. "I took your Statistics and Data Modeling courses" — clearly a former student. Check how people address each other and what they reference.

exercises.passage:
The application deadline is April 5th, so there's no rush — I just wanted to give you plenty of time. If you're comfortable being a reference, I can send you my updated resume and a brief description of the role so you have context for any questions they might ask.

Please let me know if this is something you'd be open to. I completely understand if you're too busy this semester.

Best regards,
Aisha Patel
exercises.question: How does Aisha feel about asking Professor Chen?
options: Demanding and impatient
Respectful and understanding of his time
Angry because he hasn't responded yet
Indifferent about whether he agrees or not
exercises.explanation: Technique: Identify FEELINGS through tone. "No rush," "plenty of time," "completely understand if you're too busy" — polite, respectful, considerate. NOT demanding or indifferent.

exercises.passage:
Dear Aisha,

Of course! I'd be happy to serve as a reference. You were one of the strongest students in both courses, and I remember your final project on predictive modeling being particularly impressive. Please do send your resume and the job description — it will help me tailor my recommendation to what they're looking for.

One thing: could you also remind me of ___? It's been a couple of years and I want to make sure I mention the right details.
exercises.question: Which best completes the blank?
options: your phone number and home address
the specific grades you received in my courses
which semester you took each course and your final project topic
how much the job pays and the company benefits
exercises.explanation: Technique: Reply context. The professor wants to write a SPECIFIC recommendation. He already remembers her project was impressive but needs details — which semesters and the project topic — to "mention the right details." Not grades (too impersonal) or personal info.

exercises.passage:
To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing to express my dissatisfaction with recent changes to the gym schedule at FitLife Downtown. As a member for over three years, I have always relied on the 6:00 AM spin class on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday mornings before work.

Last week, without any prior notice, this class was moved to 7:30 AM. This makes it impossible for me to attend, as I start work at 8:00 and need at least 20 minutes to shower and commute.
exercises.question: What is the main reason for this complaint?
options: The gym membership fee increased
The spin class was moved to a time that conflicts with work
The spin class instructor was replaced
The gym equipment is outdated and needs repair
exercises.explanation: Technique: Identify the Problem (Paragraph 1). The class moved from 6:00 AM to 7:30 AM, and the member starts work at 8:00. Time conflict is the core issue.

exercises.passage:
I spoke with the front desk staff, who said the change was made because only four people were attending the early class. However, I know several members who also preferred the 6:00 AM slot but were not consulted before the decision was made.

I would appreciate it if FitLife could either restore the original time or offer an alternative early-morning group fitness option. I am a loyal customer and would hate to have to find another gym over something that seems easily fixable.

Sincerely,
Daniel Reeves
exercises.question: What tone does Daniel use in his complaint?
options: Aggressive and threatening to sue
Firm but polite, offering solutions
Sarcastic and mocking
Apologetic and unsure of himself
exercises.explanation: Technique: Identify FEELINGS. Daniel is firm ("I would appreciate," "loyal customer") but constructive (offers two solutions). He's not aggressive ("would hate to") or apologetic.

exercises.passage:
Dear Mr. Reeves,

Thank you for bringing this to our attention. We understand how important your morning routine is, and we apologize for the lack of communication regarding the schedule change.

After reviewing the feedback from you and several other members, we have decided to ___. We hope this addresses your concern and look forward to continuing to serve you.

Best regards,
FitLife Management
exercises.question: Which best completes the blank?
options: permanently cancel all spin classes
offer you a 50% discount on a new gym membership
reinstate the 6:00 AM class on a trial basis and survey members about preferred times
transfer your membership to our other location across town
exercises.explanation: Technique: Reply matches complaint. Daniel asked to "restore the original time OR offer an alternative." The management response should address this directly — reinstating + surveying is the most reasonable and constructive response.

exercises.passage:
Dear Parents and Guardians,

Maple Grove Elementary is excited to announce our annual Spring Fair, scheduled for Saturday, May 10th from 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM. This year's theme is "Around the World," and each classroom will represent a different country with food, games, and cultural displays.

To make this event a success, we need parent volunteers in several areas: food preparation, game stations, face painting, and setup/cleanup crews.
exercises.question: What is the purpose of this email?
options: To announce a school closure
To invite parents to volunteer for the Spring Fair
To request donations for new playground equipment
To inform parents about a change in school policy
exercises.explanation: Technique: Identify PURPOSE from Paragraph 1. The email announces the Spring Fair AND requests volunteers. The core purpose is recruiting parent help.

exercises.passage:
Volunteers who sign up for a two-hour shift will receive a family pass worth $20 in food and game tokens. If you can commit to a four-hour shift, you'll also receive a free Spring Fair t-shirt. Please note that all food volunteers must have a valid Food Safe certificate.

To sign up, please reply to this email by April 25th with your preferred role and time slot.

Warm regards,
Ms. Patricia Liu
Spring Fair Committee Chair
exercises.question: What is required to volunteer in food preparation?
options: A minimum four-hour shift commitment
A valid Food Safe certificate
Previous experience working in a kitchen
A donation of ingredients from home
exercises.explanation: Technique: Specific detail directly stated. "All food volunteers must have a valid Food Safe certificate." Only select what you read — no assumptions about experience or donations.

exercises.passage:
Hi Ms. Liu,

I'd love to help with the Spring Fair! I'm available the whole day, so sign me up for a four-hour shift. I have my Food Safe certificate, so I'm happy to do food preparation. My daughter is in Mrs. Kim's class (Grade 3, Japan booth) and she's so excited about it.

One question — my son is only 14 months old. Would it be okay if I ___?
exercises.question: Which best completes the blank?
options: bring him along and keep him in a stroller near my station
leave him at home alone while I volunteer
ask the school to provide childcare during my shift
skip the volunteer shift entirely because of him
exercises.explanation: Technique: Reply logic. The parent is committed (available all day, four-hour shift) and just has a practical concern about a toddler. Bringing him in a stroller is the most realistic and reasonable request.

exercises.passage:
Dear Mr. Thompson,

I'm writing to you as a fellow resident of Parkview Condominiums. Over the past two weeks, there has been loud music coming from your unit most evenings between 10:00 PM and midnight. I understand that everyone has the right to enjoy their home, and I normally wouldn't bring this up.

However, my wife works early morning shifts at the hospital and needs to sleep by 9:30 PM. The bass from the music vibrates through our shared wall, making it impossible for her to fall asleep.
exercises.question: Why is the writer particularly concerned about the noise?
options: Their children cannot do homework in the evening
Their wife needs early sleep for hospital morning shifts
They are planning to sell their condo and the noise reduces value
The building rules forbid any music after 8:00 PM
exercises.explanation: Technique: Find the specific reason. "My wife works early morning shifts at the hospital and needs to sleep by 9:30 PM." The concern is specifically about her sleep schedule, not general annoyance.

exercises.passage:
I checked our building bylaws, and quiet hours begin at 10:00 PM on weeknights. I'm not asking you to stop playing music — just to lower the volume or use headphones after 10:00. This seems like a fair compromise that lets both of us enjoy our evenings.

If you'd like to discuss this in person, I'm in Unit 412. I'd rather resolve this between us than involve building management.

Respectfully,
David Park, Unit 412
exercises.question: What approach does David take with this complaint?
options: He threatens to call the police immediately
He proposes a reasonable compromise and prefers direct resolution
He demands that Mr. Thompson move to a different unit
He insists on involving building management right away
exercises.explanation: Technique: Identify TONE. David offers a compromise (lower volume or headphones after 10), references bylaws without threatening, and prefers to "resolve this between us." Firm but neighborly.

exercises.passage:
Hi David,

Thanks for reaching out directly — I really appreciate that. I honestly had no idea the bass was traveling through the wall. I've been working on a music project and completely lost track of time on those late nights.

I'll absolutely keep the volume down after 10 PM, and I just ordered a good pair of studio headphones that should arrive ___. Problem solved!
exercises.question: Which best completes the blank?
options: sometime next year when I can afford them
by Thursday, so this won't be an issue anymore
never, because I don't believe in headphones
but I'll still play music as loud as I want
exercises.explanation: Technique: Reply tone matches resolution. Mr. Thompson is apologetic and cooperative ("absolutely keep the volume down"). A quick delivery timeline (Thursday) matches his cooperative attitude. The problem is being actively solved.

exercises.passage:
Dear Conference Registrants,

Thank you for registering for the Canadian Innovation Summit 2026, taking place June 15-17 at the Metro Convention Centre in Vancouver. We are writing to confirm your registration and provide important logistical details.

Your registration includes access to all keynote sessions, panel discussions, and the networking reception on June 16th. Lunch will be provided on all three days. Please note that workshop attendance requires separate registration and an additional fee of $75 per workshop.
exercises.question: What is NOT included in the basic registration?
options: Keynote sessions
Lunch on all three days
Workshop attendance
The networking reception
exercises.explanation: Technique: Eliminate what IS included. Keynotes ✓, lunch ✓, networking ✓. Workshops require "separate registration and an additional fee." Only workshops are excluded.

exercises.passage:
The conference venue is a 10-minute walk from Waterfront Station (SkyTrain). We strongly recommend using public transit, as parking at the convention centre is limited and costs $35 per day. For those flying in, Vancouver International Airport is connected to the SkyTrain — a 25-minute ride directly to Waterfront Station.

Please wear your name badge at all times during the conference. Replacement badges can be obtained at the registration desk for a $10 fee.
exercises.question: Why does the conference recommend public transit?
options: Public transit is free for conference attendees
Parking is limited and expensive at the venue
The venue does not have any parking at all
Conference attendees receive a transit discount
exercises.explanation: Technique: Read for the stated reason. "Parking at the convention centre is limited and costs $35 per day." NOT free transit or no parking — it's limited and expensive.

exercises.passage:
Dear Conference Team,

Thank you for the information. I'm very excited about the summit! I have a question about accessibility. I use a wheelchair and wanted to confirm that all venues and session rooms are ___. I also noticed the networking reception is on June 16th — is it at the same venue or a different location?

Looking forward to it,
Meera Khatri
exercises.question: Which best completes the blank?
options: only available to VIP ticket holders
wheelchair accessible, including stage areas and restrooms
located on the second floor with no elevator
going to have live music and dancing
exercises.explanation: Technique: Reply context. Meera uses a wheelchair and is asking about accessibility. The logical completion is confirming wheelchair access to venues and facilities.

exercises.passage:
Dear Customer Service,

I purchased a wireless keyboard (Order #WK-4892) from your online store on February 28th. When it arrived on March 5th, I noticed that the spacebar sticks intermittently and the Bluetooth connection drops every few minutes. I've tried reconnecting and updating drivers, but the issues persist.

According to your website, items can be returned within 30 days for a full refund if they are defective. I would like to request either a replacement or a full refund.
exercises.question: What problems does the customer report?
options: The keyboard is the wrong color and size
The spacebar sticks and Bluetooth keeps disconnecting
The keyboard was never delivered
The packaging was damaged during shipping
exercises.explanation: Technique: Identify specific details when directly stated. TWO problems: (1) spacebar sticks, (2) Bluetooth drops. Both are clearly listed in the first paragraph.

exercises.passage:
I've attached photos of the packaging and the keyboard, as well as a screen recording showing the Bluetooth disconnection. My order confirmation and receipt are also attached for your reference.

I hope this can be resolved quickly, as I need a functioning keyboard for my remote work. I've been a satisfied customer for several years and have purchased multiple items from your store without any issues until now.

Thank you,
Robert Kim
exercises.question: What evidence does Robert provide?
options: A written letter from his employer
Photos, a screen recording, and order documentation
Testimony from a computer repair technician
A police report about the defective product
exercises.explanation: Technique: List what the text says. Photos of packaging/keyboard, screen recording of Bluetooth issue, order confirmation and receipt. Only select evidence actually mentioned.

exercises.passage:
Dear Mr. Kim,

Thank you for reaching out and for providing the detailed documentation. We're sorry to hear about the issues with your keyboard. After reviewing your case, we're happy to offer you ___. A prepaid return shipping label has been sent to your email.

We value your continued loyalty,
TechGear Support
exercises.question: Which best completes the blank?
options: a 10% discount on your next purchase only
a full refund once we receive the defective unit back
store credit that expires in 7 days
nothing, as the return window has closed
exercises.explanation: Technique: Reply matches request + policy. Robert requested "a replacement or full refund," the item is within 30 days, and they sent a return label. A full refund upon return is the logical resolution.

exercises.passage:
📋 CEDAR COMMUNITY CENTRE — SPRING CLASSES

🎨 Watercolor Painting | Tuesdays 6-8 PM | Room 201 | $85/8 weeks | Instructor: Maria Santos
🧘 Yoga for Beginners | Mondays & Wednesdays 7-8 AM | Studio B | $60/8 weeks | Instructor: David Lee
📸 Digital Photography | Thursdays 7-9 PM | Room 105 | $95/8 weeks | Instructor: Aisha Khan
🍳 Cooking Basics | Saturdays 10 AM-12 PM | Kitchen Lab | $120/8 weeks | Instructor: Chef Pierre

✅ All classes start April 7th. Registration closes March 28th.
exercises.question: Which class meets twice a week?
options: Watercolor Painting
Yoga for Beginners
Digital Photography
Cooking Basics
exercises.explanation: Technique: Find the Pattern. Most classes meet once a week. Yoga is the exception: "Mondays & Wednesdays." Look for what DIFFERS from the pattern.

exercises.passage:
From: sarah.nguyen@email.ca
To: info@cedarcc.ca
Subject: Spring Class Registration

Hi,

I'd like to register for two classes this spring. I work weekdays until 5:30 PM, so morning classes won't work for me. I'm interested in anything creative or hands-on. My budget is about $200 total for both classes.
exercises.question: Based on the diagram, which TWO classes can Sarah attend?
options: Yoga and Cooking
Watercolor and Photography
Yoga and Photography
Cooking and Watercolor
exercises.explanation: Technique: Match email to diagram. Sarah works until 5:30 (no morning classes → eliminates Yoga). Wants creative/hands-on + $200 budget. Watercolor ($85, Tue 6-8) + Photography ($95, Thu 7-9) = $180 ✓. Cooking is Sat but $120+$85=$205 over budget.

exercises.passage:
From: sarah.nguyen@email.ca
To: info@cedarcc.ca
Subject: Re: Spring Class Registration

Thank you for confirming my registration for both classes! I'm especially excited about the photography class — I just bought a new camera and have no idea how to use it properly. Quick question: do I need to bring any supplies for the painting class, or is everything provided?
exercises.question: How does Sarah feel about the photography class?
options: Anxious because she doesn't know the instructor
Excited because she wants to learn to use her new camera
Disappointed because she wanted a painting class instead
Indifferent because she only signed up to fill time
exercises.explanation: Technique: Identify FEELING from Part 2. "Especially excited" + "just bought a new camera and have no idea how to use it" — clear excitement about learning photography. Positive feeling, not anxious or indifferent.

exercises.passage:
🎬 STARLIGHT CINEMA — THIS WEEKEND

🦸 Hero Rising | Fri 7:00, 9:30 | Sat 2:00, 5:00, 8:00 | Sun 1:00, 4:00 | PG-13 | 2h 15min
🔍 The Last Clue | Fri 8:15 | Sat 3:30, 7:00 | Sun 2:30, 6:00 | R | 1h 50min
🧸 Panda Friends | Fri 6:30 | Sat 11:00, 1:30, 4:00 | Sun 11:00, 2:00 | G | 1h 25min
👻 Midnight Manor | Fri 10:00 | Sat 9:30 | Sun — | R | 2h 05min

🍿 $14.50 adults | $9.50 children (12 & under) | $11.00 seniors/students | Tuesday = $8.00 all
exercises.question: A family with two young children (ages 5 and 8) wants to watch a movie on Saturday afternoon. Which movie can they see?
options: Hero Rising (PG-13)
The Last Clue (R)
Panda Friends (G)
Midnight Manor (R)
exercises.explanation: Technique: Match constraints to diagram. Young children → needs G or PG rating. R is restricted. Saturday afternoon showing needed. Panda Friends is G-rated with Sat 11:00, 1:30, 4:00 showings. Hero Rising is PG-13 (ages 5 and 8 may not qualify).

exercises.passage:
From: jake.morrison@email.ca
To: lisa.chen@email.ca
Subject: Movie this weekend?

Hey Lisa! Want to catch a movie on Saturday? I finish work at 6:30 PM, so anything after 7 works. I'm in the mood for something thrilling — maybe that mystery movie everyone's been talking about? Or the superhero one if you prefer action.
exercises.question: Based on the showtimes, which showing works for Jake?
options: The Last Clue at Sat 3:30
Hero Rising at Sat 8:00
Panda Friends at Sat 4:00
Midnight Manor at Sat 9:30
exercises.explanation: Technique: Match email to diagram. Jake finishes at 6:30, wants after 7:00. Options: Hero Rising Sat 8:00 ✓, The Last Clue Sat 7:00 ✓, Midnight Manor 9:30 ✓. He wants "thrilling" — both Last Clue and Hero Rising work, but Hero Rising at 8:00 is the safe pick after 7:00.

exercises.passage:
From: lisa.chen@email.ca
To: jake.morrison@email.ca
Subject: Re: Movie this weekend?

Hero Rising at 8:00 sounds perfect! I've been wanting to see it since the trailer came out. My sister wants to join too — she's a university student, so she'll get the discount price. Should we grab dinner at that new Thai place before the movie? We could meet at 6:45.
exercises.question: How much will Lisa's sister pay for the ticket?
options: $14.50 (adult price)
$11.00 (student price)
$9.50 (child price)
$8.00 (Tuesday special)
exercises.explanation: Technique: Match detail to diagram. Lisa's sister is a university student. The pricing shows "$11.00 seniors/students." Saturday, not Tuesday, so $11.00 student rate applies.

exercises.passage:
🏠 DOWNTOWN APARTMENTS — AVAILABLE NOW

Unit A | 1-bed | 650 sq ft | $1,450/mo | Floor 3 | Balcony | Pet-friendly | Avail: Apr 1
Unit B | 2-bed | 850 sq ft | $1,850/mo | Floor 7 | No balcony | No pets | Avail: Apr 1
Unit C | 1-bed | 700 sq ft | $1,350/mo | Floor 2 | Balcony | No pets | Avail: May 1
Unit D | 2-bed | 900 sq ft | $1,950/mo | Floor 10 | Balcony | Pet-friendly | Avail: Apr 15

📞 All units include: gym, laundry, underground parking ($75/mo extra)
exercises.question: Which unit allows pets AND has a balcony?
options: Unit A only
Unit B only
Units A and D
Units C and D
exercises.explanation: Technique: Find the Pattern. Check both criteria: Pet-friendly + Balcony. Unit A: ✓ pets, ✓ balcony. Unit B: ✗ pets. Unit C: ✗ pets. Unit D: ✓ pets, ✓ balcony. Both A and D.

exercises.passage:
From: maria.costa@email.ca
To: rentals@downtownapts.ca
Subject: Apartment Inquiry

Hi,

I'm looking for a 2-bedroom apartment starting in April. I have a small dog, so pet-friendly is a must. My budget is up to $2,000 per month including parking. Could you tell me which unit would be best for me?
exercises.question: Which unit fits Maria's requirements?
options: Unit A — 1-bed, pet-friendly, $1,450
Unit B — 2-bed, no pets, $1,850
Unit C — 1-bed, no pets, $1,350
Unit D — 2-bed, pet-friendly, $1,950 + $75 parking = $2,025
exercises.explanation: Technique: Match ALL requirements. 2-bed + pets + April + under $2,000 with parking. Unit D is 2-bed, pet-friendly, April 15. BUT $1,950 + $75 parking = $2,025 (over budget). This is a TRAP — the correct answer acknowledges Unit D is the closest match but technically over budget. In CELPIP, the best available option is the answer.

exercises.passage:
From: rentals@downtownapts.ca
To: maria.costa@email.ca
Subject: Re: Apartment Inquiry

Hi Maria,

Thank you for your interest! Based on your needs, Unit D would be the best fit — 2 bedrooms, pet-friendly, and available April 15th. The total with parking would be $2,025. However, since you mentioned your budget is $2,000, I'd like to let you know that we offer a 5% discount for tenants who sign a 12-month lease. That would bring your total to approximately ___.
exercises.question: Which best completes the blank?
options: $1,850 per month
$1,924 per month
$2,025 per month
$2,100 per month
exercises.explanation: Technique: Simple math from context. Unit D rent $1,950 + parking $75 = $2,025. With 5% discount on rent: $1,950 × 0.95 = $1,852.50 + $75 = $1,927.50 ≈ $1,924. The discount applies to rent, not parking.

exercises.passage:
🚌 ROUTE 45 — DOWNTOWN EXPRESS

📍 Stops: Cedar Mall → Oak Street → City Hall → Central Station → Harbour Terminal

⏰ Weekday departures from Cedar Mall:
6:15 AM | 6:45 | 7:15 | 7:45 | 8:15 | Then every 30 min until 6:15 PM | 7:00 | 8:00 | 9:30 (last)

⏰ Weekend departures from Cedar Mall:
8:00 AM | Then every 45 min until 6:00 PM | 7:30 | 9:00 (last)

🕐 Travel time: Cedar Mall to Central Station = 25 min | Cedar Mall to Harbour Terminal = 35 min
exercises.question: How often does the bus run on weekdays between 8:15 AM and 6:15 PM?
options: Every 15 minutes
Every 30 minutes
Every 45 minutes
Every hour
exercises.explanation: Technique: Read diagram carefully. "8:15 | Then every 30 min until 6:15 PM." Weekday midday frequency is every 30 minutes.

exercises.passage:
From: tom.baker@email.ca
To: anna.baker@email.ca
Subject: Getting to the ferry

Hey Anna, I need to catch the 4:00 PM ferry from Harbour Terminal on Saturday. What time should I leave from Cedar Mall to make sure I get there with some time to spare? I don't want to cut it too close.
exercises.question: What is the latest bus Tom should take on Saturday to arrive by 3:45 PM?
options: The 2:30 PM bus (arrives ~3:05)
The 3:00 PM bus (arrives ~3:35)
The 3:15 PM bus (arrives ~3:50)
The 3:45 PM bus (arrives ~4:20)
exercises.explanation: Technique: Calculate from diagram. Weekend = every 45 min from 8:00. Travel to Harbour = 35 min. He wants to arrive by ~3:45 (spare time before 4:00 ferry). 3:00 bus → arrives ~3:35 ✓. 3:45 bus → arrives ~4:20 ✗ (misses ferry).

exercises.passage:
From: anna.baker@email.ca
To: tom.baker@email.ca
Subject: Re: Getting to the ferry

Take the bus that gets you there around 3:35 — that gives you 25 minutes before the ferry. Don't forget your transit card! Also, the bus can get crowded on Saturday afternoons, so you might want to be at the stop a few minutes early to make sure you get a seat.
exercises.question: What is Anna's main concern for Tom?
options: That the bus might break down on the way
That the ferry might be cancelled due to weather
That the bus could be crowded and he should arrive early at the stop
That Tom will forget to buy a ferry ticket
exercises.explanation: Technique: Comprehension of reply. Anna says "bus can get crowded on Saturday afternoons" and advises arriving early "to make sure you get a seat." Her concern is crowding, not breakdowns or cancellations.

Mushroom Risotto only

exercises.passage:

exercises.passage:

exercises.passage:

From: david.park@email.ca

exercises.question: Which combination works for David AND his daughter?

AllClear Spray for David + KidsCare for daughter
NightRest for David + KidsCare for daughter
ColdEaze for both David and his daughter

AllClear: no drowsiness ✓, congestion ✓ (but not headache). Daughter (age 4): KidsCare ✓. Best available: AllClear for David + KidsCare for daughter.

exercises.passage:
From: pharmacist@wellnessrx.ca
To: david.park@email.ca
Subject: Re: Cold medication question

Hi David,

For you, I'd recommend AllClear Nasal Spray for the congestion — it has no drowsiness side effects so it's safe for driving. For the headache, you can safely take regular acetaminophen (like Tylenol) alongside it. For your daughter, KidsCare Syrup is perfect for her age.

exercises.question: What extra advice does the pharmacist add?
options: David should stay home from work today
David should take NightRest instead of AllClear
Never give adult medication to the child, even in smaller doses
The daughter should also take AllClear spray
